title: Heliotropium rollotii
intro: Heliotropium rollotii, also known as East African heliotrope, is a plant species within the borage family, Boraginaceae, native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, which can aid in treating various conditions including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Heliotropium rollotii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Heliotropium rollotii is a species of flowering plant in the borage family, Boraginaceae. This species is native to Madagascar. Heliotropium rollotii belongs to the genus Heliotropium, which is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as turnsole or heliotrope. Heliotropium rollotii is a relatively rare and lesser-known species within the Heliotropium genus.
